demographics.living_situation: lives at home; parent present during the consultation

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Whenever I stand up, my heart races to about 140 beats a minute, my head spins and I feel like I'm going to faint, and my legs turn a purplish colour. It's been going on for months but this last week I can barely get out of bed. My mum is here with me.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It's gotten so bad this week that I can't shower standing up or stand long enough to make food without nearly passing out — I need help to be able to move around safely.

symptom_narrative.onset: started gradually about 6 months ago; much worse over the past week
symptom_narrative.character: as soon as I stand my heart pounds and races, my head goes light and spinny like I'll faint, I feel shaky and jittery, and my lower legs go a reddish-purple colour
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst it's ever been — I'm basically stuck in bed
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the racing and dizziness are all over/in my head and chest; the colour change is in my lower legs and feet
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: only happens when I stand or stay upright; comes on within a few minutes of standing
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: lying back down — the symptoms settle almost immediately when I'm flat
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: standing up, staying on my feet, and hot environments like a warm shower
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: chronic tiredness all the time, really bad exercise intolerance, and a foggy-headed feeling I call 'brain fog'; feeling trembly and jittery when it happens
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: this has been building for 6 months but I've never had it checked properly
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: currently bedbound — can't shower, can't stand to cook, and it's wrecking my university studies

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: university student living at home; parent present and helping; very anxious and distressed about how much this is disrupting her studies and daily life

objective_data_offered.value: my smartwatch shows my heart rate is around 75 when I'm lying down but it jumps to about 125 to 140 within a few minutes of standing
objective_data_offered.timing: measured repeatedly over the last few days
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: consumer wrist wearable, optical HR can be inaccurate with motion; not a clinical measurement

objective_data_offered.value: my lower legs and feet turn a reddish-purple colour after I stand for about 5 minutes, and it goes back to normal when I lie down
when I stand I feel like I'm about to faint and go shaky and trembly, but I haven't actually blacked out or fallen

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a home pulse oximeter
